description: Legal <onTimeout> declaration (§51.0.M). <Loading rule=(.Done | .TimedOut)> carries <onTimeout after=30s to=.TimedOut/>; the to= target IS in the rule= set, so the declaration compiles clean (no E-ENGINE-INVALID-TRANSITION, no E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED). RUNTIME HALF IS HARNESS-GATED: the timer->variant firing after 30s cannot be exercised because the conformance harness has NO virtual clock (driver.ts drives selector events + settle only; timers deferred to v1.next). The timer runtime is a PA/infra follow-on on a virtual-clock driver.
rationale: §51.0.M a legal <onTimeout> whose to= is in the surrounding rule= set compiles without diagnostics; the armed-on-entry / fire-after-N-ms runtime is not assertable without a virtual clock.
runtime-half-ref: virtual-clock driver (no clock in conformance/driver.ts:19-21; timers deferred to v1.next) — timer->variant fire is a PA/infra follow-on
